title: 有翼之暗
summary:
In an ornate, antique-style room, investigators examine a desk featuring an asymmetric, crooked vase. Tsubaki observes that this desk is where one of Ito's feet was hidden. Mutsu notes that the killer likely did not intend to hide the foot, as it was placed in a bottom drawer covered by documents. Among these documents, the investigators find a high-quality white envelope addressed to Mr. Motoyama of Goryokuchō. Goryokuchō is described as a famous, highly marketed private detective and one of the "Kyoto Odd Men."

Because the envelope's seal remains intact, the group concludes the killer had no interest in its contents. The investigators discuss why Ito commissioned two different detectives—Mutsuken and a detective from the Higashinari Detective Agency—simultaneously, speculating that the matter may have been exceptionally serious. Jiroguchi suggests collecting fingerprints from the letter before it is opened.

The investigation then moves to the room adjacent to Ito's, belonging to Umayo. Using a key taken from Umayo's suit, the police chief unlocks the door. Unlike the other rooms, Umayo's room shows no signs of disturbance or new bodies. However, the investigators discover an extensive collection of approximately fifty to sixty enlarged, framed photographs of horses covering the walls. The presence of these photos suggests Umayo had a deep obsession with racehorses rather than gambling. Mutoh mentions a specific horse named "Reed's Uma," noting its age, while Juchū expresses contempt for the subject of horse racing.
